query_text: Plan a 3-day trip for 4 people from Boston to Wilmington from November 2nd to November 4th, 2024, with a budget of $4,500. Include visits to sights and landmarks.

request.query: Plan a 3-day trip for 4 people from Boston to Wilmington from November 2nd to November 4th, 2024, with a budget of $4,500. Include visits to sights and landmarks.
request.persona: Traveler Type: Laidback Traveler; Purpose of Travel: Cultural Exploration; Spending Preference: Economical Traveler; Location Preference: Cities

optimization.llm_reasoning_summary: This itinerary optimizes the use of available flights, accommodations, and attractions while staying within the budget and adhering to the traveler's preferences.

days.llm_notes: Day 1 includes the outbound flight, breakfast, morning attraction, lunch, and dinner. The afternoon attraction is chosen based on proximity and visit duration.
Day 2 is a full non-travel day with breakfast, morning attraction, lunch, afternoon attraction, and dinner. The attractions are chosen based on proximity and diversity.
Day 3 includes the return flight, breakfast, morning attraction, and lunch. Dinner is skipped as the return flight is too early.

properties.description: As you walk the decks of the NORTH CAROLINA, imagine yourself in the South Pacific during WWII searching the sky for enemy aircraft, anticipating what may happen next. Step aboard the most decorated US Battleship of WWII! Explore nine decks, crew's quarters, gun turrets, and much more. Discover where history comes alive. Located at the junctions of highways 17, 76, 74, 421 on the Cape Fear River across from historic downtown Wilmington. Open every day!

properties.description: Built in 1770, the Burgwin-Wright House & Gardens is the only structure in Wilmington from the colonial era open to the public. Operated as a museum house, it offers visitors a unique opportunity to experience what life was like in the 18th century. All rooms have been restored and showcase hundreds of objects. Occupying an acre, gardens consist of seven areas, including an orchard, a kitchen garden and a rose garden. Built using the walls of a former city jail as a foundation, circa 1744, the house retains many vestiges of its previous incarnation such as outdoor and sub-basement jail cells and a freestanding kitchen house with a massive hearth complete with period cooking utensils. Guided tours include the stories about the people who lived and worked here and the women’s organization that saved this landmark from the wrecking ball. Open Mon.-Sat. 10am-4pm, guided tours are hourly with the last tour at 3pm. The visitor center houses a gift shop, art gallery and exhibit hall.

properties.name: Genki Sushi
properties.description:
NOTE****If you are reading this description, 
* Starting October 1st 2024: We will no longer be able to seat you unless your entire party has arrived and ready to sit ON TIME. We will hold your Table/Seats ONLY 10 minutes past the reservation time. *After 10 minutes, you will lose your reservation to someone who is on the waiting list. This will allow us to provide better customer service to our guests as we increase in volume, and allow the evening to flow better! 
Genki Sushi is the ONLY Authentic Japanese sushi bar and Restaurant in Wilmington and surrounding areas. We only use fresh ingredients and we make every thing in house including our very own soy sauce blend. We are located in the heart of Wilmington. We strive hard to maintain the highest quality of food, ingredients and service. We are considered " A True taste of Japan". Come and see what real Japanese sushi taste like! We cant wait for you to have a new place to eat sushi at, and that is with us!

properties.name: Front Street Brewery
properties.description: Wilmington's Original Brewery specializing in made-from-scratch dishes at affordable prices, award-winning Craft Beer, and Largest Whiskey Selection in town! A huge selection of food that ranges from sandwiches, seafood, and Southern specialties such as Shrimp & Grits. We serve a FULL menu until 10pm Sunday-Thursday and from 11:30am until 11pm Friday and Saturday. Parking available in the Market Street Parking deck with first 30 min FREE.
